, , Jie Liu, Xiaojun Liu, Lukito Muliadi, Steve Neuendorffer, John Reekie, Neil Smyth, Jeff Tsay and Yuhong Xiong ERL Technical Report UCB/ERL No. M99/37 University of California, Berkeley, CA, USA 94720 July 1999 This paper has been superseded by T

4720 July 1999 This paper has been superseded by Technical Memorandum UCB/ERL M01/11, March 6, 2001, also entitled Overview of the Ptolemy Project ABSTRACT The Ptolemy project studies heterogeneous modeling and design of concurrent systems. The f

s modeling and design of concurrent systems. The focus is on embedded systems, particularly those that mix technologies, including for example analog and digital electronics, hardware and software, and electronics and mechanical devices (including ME

d electronics and mechanical devices (including MEMS, microelectromechanical systems). The focus is also on systems that are complex in the sense that they mix widely different operations, such as signal processing, feedback control, sequential decis
nal processing, feedback control, sequential decision making, and user interfaces. Send comments to Edward A. Lee at eal@eecs.berkeley.edu .
